message:
feat(snapshot_engine): deep cx threading in run_periodic_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Tick 106's `run_periodic_with_cx` was a pre-flight-only delegate —
the scheduler loop itself used a fresh `cx::for_request()` for its
shutdown-watcher and trigger-receiver polls, so a caller that
cancelled its cx mid-flight had to wait up to `interval_seconds`
(default 30s) before the scheduler noticed.

This tick lifts that limitation via a narrow internal refactor:

1. New private `scheduler_body(&self, cx: &Cx, shutdown,
   pane_provider)` helper shared by both entry points. Uses
   `shutdown.changed(cx)` and `trigger_rx.recv(cx)` so caller
   cancellation propagates into both poll sites. Adds explicit
   `cx.checkpoint()` seams at each loop iteration.

2. `run_periodic` preserves prior semantics by passing
   `cx::for_request()` to `scheduler_body`.

3. `run_periodic_with_cx` threads the caller's cx through to the
   body — this is the behavioral upgrade.

4. A parallel `scheduler_body_legacy(&self, shutdown,
   pane_provider)` handles the non-asupersync-runtime build,
   preserving the exact prior `shutdown.changed()` /
   `trigger_rx.recv()` semantics (no cx).

Legacy `run_periodic` externally preserved unchanged.

Regression test `run_periodic_with_cx_mid_flight_cancel_exits_quickly`
pins the behavioral upgrade: start the scheduler with
interval_seconds=30, let it settle into the shutdown-watcher poll,
then cancel the caller's cx and assert the scheduler exits in
<2s (previously would have blocked up to 30s).

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 92/92
snapshot_engine tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
